name: Cushion
tagline: AI-powered bank fee negotiation and BNPL platform
description: Despite reaching $3M ARR, processing $300M+ in BNPL loans, and onboarding 1M+ users, Cushion couldn't reach the scale needed to sustain the business after 8 years.
story: Cushion was founded in 2016 as an AI-powered tool that negotiated bank fees on behalf of consumers — a genuinely clever application of automation to a universally hated problem. The company raised $21.6 million from Afore Capital, Flourish Ventures, and Vestigo Ventures, reaching a post-money valuation of $82.4 million in 2022. Cushion later pivoted into buy-now-pay-later, processing over $300 million in loans and onboarding more than one million consumers. It reached $3 million in annual recurring revenue. But after eight years and multiple pivots, $3 million ARR wasn't enough to sustain the burn rate or justify additional investment. Cushion shut down in late 2024 — a startup that found users, found revenue, but never found enough of either to survive.
